raw_consumer_statement:
I am disputing fraudulent entertainment-related charges on my Macys/American Express account that I did not authorize. These charges began in XX/XX/2023, and despite canceling the card and repeated assurances that an investigation was underway, no resolution has been reached. 

The charges, totaling over {$3000.00}, are unusual because my physical card was never usedI always swipe my card for transactions. Additionally, the statements are vague and only list the charges under the generic entertainment category, with no specific merchant names. The charges also span multiple days and appear from the same merchant, further suggesting fraud. 

For comparison, a similar issue involving fraudulent charges was quickly resolved by XXXX on my XXXX XXXX card, demonstrating that the fraud on my accounts is valid and not an isolated incident. However, Macys/American Express continues to charge interest on these transactions, even after the card was canceled. 

This has been ongoing for nearly a year and has negatively impacted my credit report due to adverse actions. I believe it is improper to charge interest on a canceled card, especially in cases involving clear fraud, and I am requesting an immediate resolution to this issue.
